MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-05-2020 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Off Calendar

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Gudetas Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Requests For Production
Off Calendar Mar-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Noah A. Phillips, esq. (415-988-9828) of Altair Law for Plaintiff/Moving party.
Natasha K. Zaslove, esq. (415-433-0990) of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP for Defendant, Uber Technology Inc. via Court Call.
Edgar Cruz, esq. (562-216-5000) of Clinton & Clinton for Defendant, Alemtsehay Gudeta via Court Call.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs IGNACIO GUTIERREZ SANCHEZ, and MARIA CHAVEZ ALVAREZ's Motion To Compel Defendant Gudetas Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Requests For Production is under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Gudetas Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Requests For Production
Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To Mar-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF IGNACIO GUTIERREZ SANCHEZ, MARIA CHAVEZ ALVAREZ's Motion To Compel Defendant Gudetas Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Requests For Production.

Continued to March 16, 2020 on the court's motion.

Judge Pro Tem: None, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Feb-21-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Noah A. Phillips, Esq. of ALTAIR LAW, T:415.988.9828, for plaintiff/moving party;
Natasha K. Zaslove, Esq. of WILSON ELSER, T:415.625.9256, for defendant Uber, etc.;
Edgar Cruz, Esq. of CLINTON & CLINTON, T:562.216.5000, for defendant Gudeta.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS IGNACIO GUTIERREZ SANCHEZ, MARIA CHAVEZ ALVAREZ's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION as follows:

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication of defendants' affirmative defenses is denied in its entirety. Plaintiff moves for summary adjudication on the ground that defendants' responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1 are factually devoid. (Pl's. MPA 4:6-10.) Since Plaintiff filed this motion, however, both defendants amended their responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1; thus, Plaintiff's motion is moot. (Gilardi Decl., Ex. A; Cruz Dec., Ex. G.) (See Code of Civ. Proc. 2030.310(a) ["Without leave of court, a party may serve an amended answer to any interrogatory that contains information subsequently discovered, inadvertently omitted, or mistakenly stated in the initial interrogatory."]; compare Union Bank v. Superior Court (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 580 [summary judgment properly granted where plaintiffs failed to file amended interrogatory answers as permitted] with Ahn v. Kumho Tire USA, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 133, 135-136 [reversing summary judgment where, in response to defendants' motion based on factually devoid responses to interrogatories, plaintiff adduced amended responses to initial discovery responses and a declaration explaining the evidentiary basis of plaintiffs' claims].)

Plaintiff's belated attempt on reply to submit a voluminous amount of additional evidence-a large binder containing 16 exhibits comprising many hundreds of pages of deposition transcripts and exhibits and other documentary evidence-is improper. (See Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537 ["The general rule of motion practice, which applies here, is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers. This principle is most prominent in the context of summary judgment motions . . . '[T]he inclusion of additional evidentiary matter with the reply should only be allowed in the exceptional case . . .'"].) The evidence proffered by plaintiff on reply is stricken.

The court agrees that many of defendants' 62 affirmative defenses are not, in fact, affirmative defenses at all, or lack merit on their face. (To cite only a single example, plaintiff did not "assume the risk" of getting hit by a motor vehicle by standing on the sidewalk.) Nonetheless, even had defendants not amended their discovery responses, adjudicating plaintiff's motion would be a pointless waste of the court's resources, especially considering defendants raise triable issues of fact as to certain of those defenses and the factual issues bearing on plaintiff's claims and any affirmative defenses necessarily will be resolved at trial.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
